candidates.label: Injury
candidates.definition: An injury refers to damage or harm caused to the structure or function of the body, either due to external forces or internal causes. Injuries can be physical or emotional and can result from accidents, falls, hits, weapons, and other causes, leading to pain, discomfort, or disability.

candidates.label: Personal Injury Ruleset
candidates.definition: A Personal Injury Ruleset is a set of legal rules and guidelines that specifically pertain to cases involving physical or emotional harm caused by another party's negligence or intentional actions.
synonyms: Conjunto de regras de lesões pessoais
Ensemble de règles sur les dommages corporels
人身傷害ルールセット

candidates.label: Personal Injury Claims
candidates.definition: Personal Injury Claims are civil claims or tort claims seeking compensation for physical or emotional harm caused by the negligence or intentional actions of another party, including but not limited to slip-and-fall accidents, medical malpractice, and motor vehicle accidents.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Law
candidates.definition: Law covering all the aspects of registering and titling motor vehicles, and licensing drivers.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Asset
candidates.definition: A Motor Vehicle is a self-propelled vehicle that runs on the surface and not on rails, primarily used for the transportation of people or goods. Types include cars, trucks, motorcycles, and buses, and they are usually powered by internal combustion engines or electric motors. This item is relevant to several areas of law (e.g., Bankruptcy).

candidates.label: Driver
candidates.definition: A driver is an individual who operates a motor vehicle, controlling its direction and speed on public or private roads. This role requires adherence to traffic laws and regulations to ensure safety and order on thoroughfares.

candidates.label: Automobile Insurance Policy
candidates.definition: An automobile insurance policy is a contract between a policyholder and an insurance company that provides financial protection against losses arising from accidents, theft, or other events affecting a motor vehicle. The policy specifies the types of coverage included, such as liability, collision, and comprehensive, and requires the payment of premiums to maintain this coverage.

candidates.label: Traffic Accident Indicator
candidates.definition: Y/N - True if an incident involved a traffic accident; false otherwise.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Law
candidates.definition: Law covering all the aspects of registering and titling motor vehicles, and licensing drivers.

candidates.label: Driver's License
candidates.definition: A driver's license is a legal document issued by governmental authorities that authorizes an individual to operate a motor vehicle on public roads. The license is typically granted after the individual has passed both written and practical driving tests, and it may come with restrictions based on age, type of vehicle, or other factors.

candidates.label: Traffic Accident Indicator
candidates.definition: Y/N - True if an incident involved a traffic accident; false otherwise.

candidates.label: Patient
candidates.definition: A Patient is an individual who receives medical care, treatment, or other health services from a healthcare provider or institution. This person is under professional care due to illness, injury, or for preventive health measures, and is the recipient of medical attention, diagnosis, and therapeutic interventions.

candidates.label: Insurance Carrier Bad Faith
candidates.definition: The concept of Insurance Carrier Bad Faith refers to the intentional or reckless failure of an insurance company to fulfill its contractual obligations to its policyholders, such as denying a valid claim or failing to investigate a claim in a timely manner.

candidates.label: Landlord Tenant Law
candidates.definition: Law that details the rights and duties of landlords and tenants
synonyms: Direito do locador e do locatário

candidates.label: General Business Liability Insurance Policy
candidates.definition: General Business Liability Insurance, also known simply as general liability insurance, provides coverage for common business risks, including bodily injury and property damage that occur at the business premises or as a result of business operations. It also often covers legal costs related to these risks. This type of insurance is foundational for most businesses.

candidates.label: Tenant
candidates.definition: A person or entity who leases, rents, or occupies property (from a landlord).

candidates.label: Slip-and-Fall Negligence
candidates.definition: Slip-and-Fall Negligence refers to a legal claim made by an individual who has suffered a personal injury due to the negligence of a property owner or occupier in maintaining safe conditions on their premises, specifically related to slipping or falling.
synonyms: Negligência em casos de escorregão e queda
Négligence en cas de glissade et de chute
すべり落ちることと転倒の過失

candidates.label: Negligence
candidates.definition: Negligence is a legal concept that refers to the failure to exercise reasonable care, resulting in harm or injury to another person or property.

candidates.label: Negligence Claims
candidates.definition: Negligence Claims refer to civil claims arising from a failure to exercise reasonable care, resulting in harm or injury to another person or property.

candidates.label: Dogs
candidates.definition: Dogs are domesticated mammals belonging to the species Canis lupus familiaris, known for their loyalty, intelligence, and diverse roles in human society. They serve as companions, working animals, and service animals, and come in a wide variety of breeds with specific traits and purposes.

candidates.label: Bite Mark Evidence
candidates.definition: Bite mark evidence involves the study of patterns left by teeth on various materials, often skin. Forensic odontologists analyze these marks to identify unique dental characteristics, potentially linking a suspect to an injury or crime scene.

candidates.label: Physician
candidates.definition: A physician or doctor is a person who is licensed to practice medicine and provide medical treatment to patients.

candidates.label: Medical Malpractice
candidates.definition: Medical Malpractice: Negligence or breach of duty by a healthcare professional that results in harm or injury to a patient.

candidates.label: Medical Malpractice Claims
candidates.definition: Medical Malpractice Claims refer to legal claims made against medical professionals or institutions for negligence or failure to provide adequate care resulting in harm or injury to a patient.

candidates.label: Medical Malpractice Defenses
candidates.definition: Medical Malpractice Defenses are legal arguments or strategies used by defendants in a medical malpractice case to defend against the plaintiff's claims of negligence or wrongdoing.
synonyms: Defesas em casos de negligência médica
Défenses en cas de faute médicale

candidates.label: Malpractice Claims
candidates.definition: Malpractice Claims refer to civil claims of negligence or malfeasance brought against a professional or tradesperson, such as a doctor, lawyer, accountant, or architect, for failing to meet the standard of care expected in their field.

candidates.label: Legal Malpractice
candidates.definition: Legal Malpractice is the failure of an attorney to provide competent and professional legal services to a client, resulting in harm or damages to the client.

candidates.label: Physician
candidates.definition: A physician or doctor is a person who is licensed to practice medicine and provide medical treatment to patients.

candidates.label: Medical Malpractice
candidates.definition: Medical Malpractice: Negligence or breach of duty by a healthcare professional that results in harm or injury to a patient.

candidates.label: Medical Malpractice Claims
candidates.definition: Medical Malpractice Claims refer to legal claims made against medical professionals or institutions for negligence or failure to provide adequate care resulting in harm or injury to a patient.

candidates.label: Medical Malpractice Defenses
candidates.definition: Medical Malpractice Defenses are legal arguments or strategies used by defendants in a medical malpractice case to defend against the plaintiff's claims of negligence or wrongdoing.
synonyms: Defesas em casos de negligência médica
Défenses en cas de faute médicale

candidates.label: Malpractice Claims
candidates.definition: Malpractice Claims refer to civil claims of negligence or malfeasance brought against a professional or tradesperson, such as a doctor, lawyer, accountant, or architect, for failing to meet the standard of care expected in their field.

candidates.label: Legal Malpractice
candidates.definition: Legal Malpractice is the failure of an attorney to provide competent and professional legal services to a client, resulting in harm or damages to the client.

candidates.label: Wrongful Death Claim
candidates.definition: A wrongful death claim is a legal action brought by the survivors of a deceased person against the party responsible for the death, seeking compensation for damages resulting from the loss of the individual's life.

candidates.label: Loss of Consortium
candidates.definition: The concept of Loss of Consortium refers to the damages suffered by a spouse or parent due to the injury or death of their loved one, which includes the loss of companionship, affection, and support.

candidates.label: Product Liability Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to liability for injuries caused by defective or unsafe products.
synonyms: Droit de la responsabilité du produit

candidates.label: Product Liability Claims
candidates.definition: Product Liability Claims refer to civil tort claims that arise when a consumer is injured or suffers damages due to a defective or dangerous product, and seeks compensation from the manufacturer or seller of the product.

candidates.label: Strict Product Liability
candidates.definition: Strict Product Liability is a legal concept that holds manufacturers and sellers responsible for any harm caused by their products, regardless of fault or negligence, if the product is found to be defective.

candidates.label: Product Liability Defenses
candidates.definition: Product Liability Defenses refer to the legal arguments and strategies used by defendants in a product liability case to defend against claims of liability for harm caused by their product, including arguments related to the inherent safety of the product, the scope of its intended use, the knowledge and experience of the user, and the balance of risks and benefits associated with the product.
synonyms: Défenses en matière de responsabilité du produit
הגנות בפשיטת רגל בגין אחריות מוצר

candidates.label: Product Liability Representation
candidates.definition: Product Liability Representation is a clause in merger and acquisition agreements where the selling party attests to the absence of product defects or claims arising from sold or distributed products of the target company. It serves to protect the buyer from potential undisclosed liabilities or claims related to product malfunctions, damages, or injuries that may arise after the acquisition.

candidates.label: Product Liability Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to liability for injuries caused by defective or unsafe products.
synonyms: Droit de la responsabilité du produit

candidates.label: Product Liability Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to liability for injuries caused by defective or unsafe products.
synonyms: Droit de la responsabilité du produit

candidates.label: Insurance Claims Form
candidates.definition: An Insurance Claims Form is a standardized document used by a policyholder to request reimbursement or coverage for a loss or event that is insured under their policy. The form usually includes fields for providing details about the claim such as the incident, damages, and other relevant documentation.

candidates.label: Claims Agent
candidates.definition: A claims agent is a person who acts on behalf of an individual or organization to manage and process insurance claims.
synonyms: クレームエージェント

candidates.label: Defamation
candidates.definition: Defamation is a civil claim and tort claim that involves the communication of a false statement that harms the reputation of an individual or entity.

candidates.label: Mass Torts Law
candidates.definition: Mass torts law is an area of law that deals with legal actions involving numerous plaintiffs against one or a few defendants, where the plaintiffs allege harm caused by common actions or products. These cases often involve complex negotiations and litigation due to the large number of claimants, extensive evidence, and significant legal and financial stakes.

candidates.label: Defamation Law
candidates.definition: The action of damaging the good reputation of someone; slander or libel.

candidates.label: Defamation
candidates.definition: Defamation is a civil claim and tort claim that involves the communication of a false statement that harms the reputation of an individual or entity.

candidates.label: Class Action Notice
candidates.definition: A Class Action Notice is a formal written communication that informs a group of people that they are part of a legal action and provides them with information about their rights and options in the case.

candidates.label: Class Representative
candidates.definition: The party or parties in a class action lawsuit who represents the interests of the entire class of similarly situated individuals or entities. This representative is responsible for acting on behalf of the class in legal proceedings and making decisions that impact all class members.

candidates.label: Class
candidates.definition: All those persons in the same category, level of rights (e.g. heirs of dead person who are related by the same degree), or who were injured by the same incident (e.g., class action lawsuit). Whether a person is part of a class often determines who can sue on behalf of the people who have been similarly damaged or collect his/her share if a class action judgment is entered.

candidates.label: Class Action [Certified Class]
candidates.definition: A Class Action is certified class consisting of a group of people who have suffered similar harm or injury, where the claims are too numerous or impractical to be brought individually.

candidates.label: Class Action Claim
candidates.definition: A Class Action Claim is a type of civil claim where a group of individuals collectively bring a lawsuit against a defendant, typically seeking compensation for damages or harm caused by the defendant's actions.
